instructions: Claustodian is a provenance-strict dataset: its data must come ONLY from official public sources (the Claude Code changelog and officially published release binaries), never leaked or non-public material. Guard that invariant. Question every "temporary" workaround that has outlived its excuse.

rules.rule: Data may originate ONLY from the official CHANGELOG.md and officially published release binaries (GitHub release assets, npm-published bundles). Flag any code path that could ingest leaked, source-map-derived, or otherwise non-public material, or that weakens the changelog-vs-binary provenance separation.

JSON under data/ is the generated source of truth, produced by `npm run scrape`, and YAML/TOML are build artifacts. Changes to symbol data belong in the scraper/schema, not in hand-edited data files. Don't suggest editing generated output directly.

CLI entrypoints are tested in-process by calling the exported main() with process.argv set and temp dirs (see the *.cli.test.ts files) so v8 coverage counts them. Prefer that pattern over spawning subprocesses, which get zero coverage credit and can hit the network.
